mission.title: Install and Configure Langflow
mission.description: Langflow is a powerful platform for building and deploying AI-powered agents and workflows. It provides a visual authoring experience and built-in API servers, making it suitable for developers looking to integrate AI workflows into applications. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes management and potential complexity in configuration.

steps.title: Install the Langflow CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Langflow command-line interface, which is necessary for managing Langflow deployments.

```bash

pip install langflow==1.7.3

```

> Ensure Python 3.10-3.13 is installed.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in the desired region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create langflow-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust region as needed.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local environment to use the new Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials langflow-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This sets the context for kubectl.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks that the nodes in your cluster are running and ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Deploy Langflow
steps.description:
This command deploys Langflow on your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add langflow https://charts.langflow.org

helm install langflow langflow/langflow --version 1.7.3 --namespace langflow --create-namespace

```

> Add the Langflow Helm repository.
> Deploy Langflow in its own namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for Langflow.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/langflow-ai/langflow/main/deploy/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/langflow-ai/langflow/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Ensure autoscaling is set up.
> Set up monitoring for Langflow.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Langflow
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Langflow deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall langflow --namespace langflow

gcloud container clusters delete langflow-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> This removes the Langflow release.
> This deletes the entire cluster and all resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Langflow
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. This process includes draining nodes if necessary.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade langflow langflow/langflow --version 1.7.3 --namespace langflow

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

```

> Replace <node-name> with the actual node name.
> Upgrade to the latest version.
> Replace <node-name> with the actual node name to allow scheduling.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates that the kubeconfig is not set up correctly or the cluster is down.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials langflow-cluster --region=us-central1' to reset kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Release langflow not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Langflow release has not been installed in the specified namespace.

**Fix:**
Ensure you have run 'helm install langflow langflow/langflow --version 1.7.3 --namespace langflow --create-namespace'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough resources allocated.

**Fix:**
Increase the number of nodes or the machine type in your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Pod is CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates an issue with the application configuration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' to diagnose the issue.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new AI models and improved the visual builder interface.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME                 STATUS   ROLES    AGE   VERSION' with all nodes in 'Ready' status.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
